• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Factorial experiment example - weight gain

Problem

(source: Crawley, The R book)

Our example comes from a farm-scale trial of animal diets. There are two factors: diet and supplement.

Diet is a factor with three levels: barley, oats and wheat.

Supplement is a factor with four levels: agrimore, control, supergain and supersupp.

The response variable is weight gain after 6 weeks.

```r
weights <- read.csv(\data/growth2.txt\, sep=\\t\)
head(weights)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

```r
```r
table(weights$diet, weights$supplement)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

     agrimore control supergain supersupp

barley 4 4 4 4 oats 4 4 4 4 wheat 4 4 4 4

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxud2l0aCh3ZWlnaHRzLFxuICAgICBjb3Bsb3QoZ2FpbiB+IGRpZXR8c3VwcGxlbWVudCxyb3dzPTIpKVxuYGBgIn0= -->

```r
with(weights,
     coplot(gain ~ diet|supplement,rows=2))
Errore in plot.window(xlim, ylim) : i valori 'xlim' devono essere finiti

We will perform an analysis of variance including a factor for the interaction between diet and supplement:

```r
model<-aov(gain~diet*supplement, data = weights)
summary(model)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgIERmIFN1bSBTcSBNZWFuIFNxIEYgdmFsdWUgICBQcig+RikgICAgXG5kaWV0ICAgICAgICAgICAgIDIgMjg3LjE3ICAxNDMuNTkgICA4My41MiAzLjAwZS0xNCAqKipcbnN1cHBsZW1lbnQgICAgICAgMyAgOTEuODggICAzMC42MyAgIDE3LjgyIDIuOTVlLTA3ICoqKlxuZGlldDpzdXBwbGVtZW50ICA2ICAgMy40MSAgICAwLjU3ICAgIDAuMzMgICAgMC45MTcgICAgXG5SZXNpZHVhbHMgICAgICAgMzYgIDYxLjg5ICAgIDEuNzIgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICBcbi0tLVxuU2lnbmlmLiBjb2RlczogIDAg4oCYKioq4oCZIDAuMDAxIOKAmCoq4oCZIDAuMDEg4oCYKuKAmSAwLjA1IOKAmC7igJkgMC4xIOKAmCDigJkgMVxuIn0= -->

            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)

diet 2 287.17 143.59 83.52 3.00e-14 supplement 3 91.88 30.63 17.82 2.95e-07 diet:supplement 6 3.41 0.57 0.33 0.917
Residuals 36 61.89 1.72
— Signif. codes: 0 ‘’ 0.001 ‘’ 0.01 ‘’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1




<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


The ANOVA table shows that there is no hint of any interaction between the two
explanatory variables (p=0.917): the effects of diet and supplement are additive.    

Let's check for this graphically...


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->

<!-- rnb-text-begin -->



Since interactions are not significant, we can consider simplifying our model:


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVjM1Z0YldGeWVTaHRiMlJsYkRJcFhHNWNibUJnWUNKOSAtLT5cblxuYGBgclxuc3VtbWFyeShtb2RlbDIpXG5cbmBgYFxuXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtZW5kIC0tPlxuIn0= -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuc3VtbWFyeShtb2RlbDIpXG5cbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
summary(model2)

            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
diet         2 287.17  143.59   92.36 4.20e-16 ***
supplement   3  91.88   30.63   19.70 3.98e-08 ***
Residuals   42  65.30    1.55                     
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Which one is best?

The second model is much simpler than the first one:

summary.lm(mod)


Call:
aov(formula = gain ~ diet * supplement, data = weights)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-2.48756 -1.00368 -0.07452  1.03496  2.68069 

Coefficients:
                              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)                    26.3485     0.6556  40.191  < 2e-16 ***
dietoats                       -3.0501     0.9271  -3.290 0.002248 ** 
dietwheat                      -6.7094     0.9271  -7.237 1.61e-08 ***
supplementcontrol              -3.0518     0.9271  -3.292 0.002237 ** 
supplementsupergain            -3.8824     0.9271  -4.187 0.000174 ***
supplementsupersupp            -0.7732     0.9271  -0.834 0.409816    
dietoats:supplementcontrol      0.2471     1.3112   0.188 0.851571    
dietwheat:supplementcontrol     0.8183     1.3112   0.624 0.536512    
dietoats:supplementsupergain    0.2470     1.3112   0.188 0.851652    
dietwheat:supplementsupergain   1.2557     1.3112   0.958 0.344601    
dietoats:supplementsupersupp   -0.6650     1.3112  -0.507 0.615135    
dietwheat:supplementsupersupp   0.8024     1.3112   0.612 0.544381    
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 1.311 on 36 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8607,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.8182 
F-statistic: 20.22 on 11 and 36 DF,  p-value: 3.295e-12
```r
summary.lm(model2)

Call: aov(formula = gain ~ diet + supplement, data = weights)

Residuals: Min 1Q Median 3Q Max -2.30792 -0.85929 -0.07713 0.92052 2.90615

Coefficients: Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept) 23.0302 0.4408 52.242 < 2e-16 dietbarley 3.0928 0.4408 7.016 1.38e-08 dietwheat -2.8975 0.4408 -6.573 5.96e-08 supplementcontrol -2.6967 0.5090 -5.298 4.03e-06 supplementsupergain -3.3815 0.5090 -6.643 4.72e-08 *** supplementsupersupp -0.7274 0.5090 -1.429 0.16
— Signif. codes: 0 ‘’ 0.001 ‘’ 0.01 ‘’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 1.247 on 42 degrees of freedom Multiple R-squared: 0.8531, Adjusted R-squared: 0.8356 F-statistic: 48.76 on 5 and 42 DF, p-value: < 2.2e-16

<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


We can compare them directly:

<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dVlXNXZkbUVvYlc5a1pXd3NiVzlrWld3eUtWeHVZR0JnWEc1Z1lHQWlmUT09IC0tPlxuXG5gYGByXG5gYGByXG5hbm92YShtb2RlbCxtb2RlbDIpXG5gYGBcbmBgYFxuXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtZW5kIC0tPlxuIn0= -->

```r
```r
anova(model,model2)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiQW5hbHlzaXMgb2YgVmFyaWFuY2UgVGFibGVcblxuTW9kZWwgMTogZ2FpbiB+IGRpZXQgKiBzdXBwbGVtZW50XG5Nb2RlbCAyOiBnYWluIH4gZGlldCArIHN1cHBsZW1lbnRcbiAgUmVzLkRmICAgIFJTUyBEZiBTdW0gb2YgU3EgICAgICBGIFByKD5GKVxuMSAgICAgMzYgNjEuODkwICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgXG4yICAgICA0MiA2NS4yOTYgLTYgICAtMy40MDU4IDAuMzMwMiAwLjkxNjZcbiJ9 -->

Analysis of Variance Table

Model 1: gain ~ diet * supplement Model 2: gain ~ diet + supplement Res.Df RSS Df Sum of Sq F Pr(>F) 1 36 61.890
2 42 65.296 -6 -3.4058 0.3302 0.9166




<!-- rnb-output-end -->

<!-- rnb-chunk-end -->


<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


The second model is simpler and it is not significantly worst than the first model, so we go for model2 and we try to interpret it.

Go back and look again at the summary of model2:   

* the barley and wheat diet are statistically different, in terms of weight gain, from the oats diet, but what about the difference between wheat and barley?
* it's the same for the control and supergain supplements with respect to agrimore, but what about the difference between them? and with supersupp??

Let's check with a TukeyHSD test:


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dVZIVnJaWGxJVTBRb2JXOWtaV3d5S1Z4dVlHQmdYRzVnWUdBaWZRPT0gLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmBgYHJcblR1a2V5SFNEKG1vZGVsMilcbmBgYFxuYGBgXG5cbjwhLS0gcm5iLXNvdXJjZS1lbmQgLS0+XG4ifQ== -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuVHVrZXlIU0QobW9kZWwyKVxuYGBgXG5gYGAifQ== -->

```r
```r
TukeyHSD(model2)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->


<!-- rnb-output-end -->

Tukey multiple comparisons of means 95% family-wise confidence level

Fit: aov(formula = gain ~ diet + supplement, data = weights)

$diet diff lwr upr p adj barley-oats 3.092817 2.021817 4.163817 0e+00 wheat-oats -2.897481 -3.968481 -1.826481 2e-07 wheat-barley -5.990298 -7.061298 -4.919297 0e+00

$supplement diff lwr upr p adj control-agrimore -2.6967005 -4.0583332 -1.3350677 0.0000234 supergain-agrimore -3.3814586 -4.7430914 -2.0198259 0.0000003 supersupp-agrimore -0.7273521 -2.0889849 0.6342806 0.4888738 supergain-control -0.6847581 -2.0463909 0.6768746 0.5400389 supersupp-control 1.9693484 0.6077156 3.3309811 0.0020484 supersupp-supergain 2.6541065 1.2924737 4.0157392 0.0000307

<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuIyBwbG90X21vZGVsKG1vZGVsMiwgdHlwZSA9IFwiZGlhZ1wiKVxucGxvdF9tb2RlbChtb2QyLCB0eXBlPVwiZWZmXCIpXG5cbmBgYCJ9 -->

```r
# plot_model(model2, type = "diag")
plot_model(mod2, type="eff")

Errore: `terms` needs to be a character vector with at least one predictor name: one term
  used for the x-axis, more optional terms as grouping factors.

check here for an example with a significant interaction: https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/jtools/vignettes/categorical.html

Interactions with at least one continuous predictor

(source: https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/jtools/vignettes/interactions.html)

This famous (Fisher’s or Anderson’s) iris data set gives the measurements in centimeters of the variables sepal length and width and petal length and width, respectively, for 50 flowers from each of 3 species of iris. The species are Iris setosa, versicolor, and virginica.

```r
data(iris)
head(iris)

<!-- rnb-source-end -->

<!-- rnb-text-begin -->


Please note that here we use `lm` rather than `aov`:


<!-- rnb-text-end -->


<!-- rnb-chunk-begin -->


<!-- rnb-output-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtYmVnaW4gZXlKa1lYUmhJam9pWUdCZ2NseHVZR0JnY2x4dVptbDBhWEpwY3lBOExTQnNiU2hRWlhSaGJDNU1aVzVuZEdnZ2ZpQlFaWFJoYkM1WGFXUjBhQ0FxSUZOd1pXTnBaWE1zSUdSaGRHRWdQU0JwY21sektWeHViR2xpY21GeWVTaHFkRzl2YkhNcFhHNXdiRzkwWDIxdlpHVnNLR1pwZEdseWFYTXNJSFI1Y0dVOVhGeHBiblJjWENsY2JtQmdZRnh1WUdCZ0luMD0gLS0+XG5cbmBgYHJcbmBgYHJcbmZpdGlyaXMgPC0gbG0oUGV0YWwuTGVuZ3RoIH4gUGV0YWwuV2lkdGggKiBTcGVjaWVzLCBkYXRhID0gaXJpcylcbmxpYnJhcnkoanRvb2xzKVxucGxvdF9tb2RlbChmaXRpcmlzLCB0eXBlPVxcaW50XFwpXG5gYGBcbmBgYFxuXG48IS0tIHJuYi1zb3VyY2UtZW5kIC0tPlxuIn0= -->


<!-- rnb-source-begin eyJkYXRhIjoiYGBgclxuYGBgclxuZml0aXJpcyA8LSBsbShQZXRhbC5MZW5ndGggfiBQZXRhbC5XaWR0aCAqIFNwZWNpZXMsIGRhdGEgPSBpcmlzKVxubGlicmFyeShqdG9vbHMpXG5wbG90X21vZGVsKGZpdGlyaXMsIHR5cGU9XFxpbnRcXClcbmBgYFxuYGBgIn0= -->

```r
```r
fitiris <- lm(Petal.Length ~ Petal.Width * Species, data = iris)
library(jtools)
plot_model(fitiris, type=\int\)

```
